name: zloss_plus_nuclear_norm
label: stage3_original
why: Cross-surface compound: z-loss (logit output regularization) + nuclear norm (weight structure regularization). These attack different ends of the forward pass — one shapes the output distribution, the other shapes the weight matrices. If both help individually, they should compose because they're mechanistically independent.
validates: Two novel regularizers from different surfaces compose additively.
falsifies: Over-regularization: both constrain the model and the compound loses capacity. Or one subsumes the other.
notes: Only interpretable after H1 and H3 solo results are in.
If H6 > max(H1, H3): compound works, different surfaces stack.
If H6 < max(H1, H3): over-regularization, pick the better solo.
